California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
october 7, L99L
Steven R. Meyers
MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & WEST
Gateway Plaza
777 Davis Street
San Leandro, California 
94577
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. I 91-41I
Dear Mr. Meyers:
You have requested advice concerning apPlication of the
conf I ict-of - inteiest provisions of the PoIitical Reform Act (the
rrgs;rr1.1 This letter is based upon the facts provided in your
Ietter and confirms the teLePhone conversations I had with Mr'
steven Mattas of your office on SePtember 24, l99]. and october 1,
1991.
our advice is Iirnited only to provisions of the Act' 
we
cannot provide advice about other conf l ict-of- interest lar^rs, such
as covernment code Section 1090.
OUESTI ON
If citation Homes pays for or reirnburses the city for the
cost of airfare, lodqing, meals and incidental expenses to enable
a committee consisting of city councilmenbers, planning
commissioners and staif to vier+ a residential developnent project
similar to the one which citation Homes is proposinq to build in
the city:
1. Will these city public officials be requj-red to report the
paid expenses on their statements of economic interests filed
pursuant to the Act?
I cor"r.,."nt code sections 81ooo-91015. A1l' statutory references
are to the Government Code unleSS otherwise indicated. 
Cornmission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations. Section
18OOo, et ses. AII references to regulat.ions are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O? o Sacramento CA 95804-0807 . (916)322'5660


Our File No. 
■ 9■-4■■
Page 2
2. will these city public officials 
be disqualj'fied from
participating in any decisions involving land use natters in which
Citation Hornes is an aPPlicant?
CONCLUS ION
The airfare, Iodging, neals and other incidental expenses
provided by Citation Hones are deerned to be gifts to the city and
not to the public officials 
if they are provided in conformance
with the criteria in In re Stone (L977) 3 FPPC ops. 52.2 Any
benefit received by the public officials is then considered
reirnbursernent of expenses. Under such circurnstances:
1. The public officials are not required to report the cost of
airfare, neals, Iodging and incidental expenses on their
statements of economic interests.
2. Since the costs of airfare, neals, lodging and incidental
expenses are not gifts to the public officials, 
they are not
coirsidered financial interests rrhich give rise to
disqual ification.
FACTS
Your client, the city of san Leandro, is currently
considering an application for a residential planned unit-
developrnenf of approximately 8oO dwelling units on approximately
100 acies of lani. 
The real property is zoned consistent with the
city'sgeneralplanwhichpermitstheresidentialdevelopment'
Acommitteeofthecitycouncil,cityplanningcornmissionand
staff wiII travel to Southein California to meet with architects
ina-iuna tl"tn.t= 
of projects which have successfully dealt with
sone of tire planning issues which this project poses' The
comnittee wiil also-neet with consultants for the property owner'
Citation Hones .
citation Homes has offered to defray aIl expenses the city
wilt incur for thj,s trip, including airfare, lodging, meal's and
other incidental expenses.
')-
' 
copies of comml'ss j'on regulations and,opinions are available in
^u"y-iir- 
f ibrar ie= . Alteriatively, 
copies of these rnaterials and
Comiission advice letters may be obtainea from the commission at a
cost of 10Q Per Page-


Our File No. 1 91-4■
■
Page 3
ANALYSIS
Your question is whether the reimbursement of the cost of
airfare, meats, lodging and incidental expenses by Citation Homes
to the city witl constitute giftsr to the councilmembers,
planning commissioners, and staff. 
If they are gifts valued
tunulatively at S25o or more, the counci lmernbers , planning
commissioners, and staff would have to disqualify thenselves fron
any decisions which witl have a reasonably foreseeable material
fiiancial effect on Citation Hones which is distinguishable fron
the effect on the public Aenerally. (Section 87103(e).) Gifts of
S50 or rnore are reportable on financial disclosure statements.
(Section 87207 (a) ( 1) . )
fn Stone, the comnission concluded that in certain situations
a gift may Ue nade to a city without providing any significant or
unrjsual blnefit to the public official. 
Under such circumstances,
the public official 
has no reporting obligations since.any benefit
he or she receives, although free of charge to the official 
and to
the city, rtwould be analogous to reinbursement for expenses or per
diem fron a state or local governlflent agency, items which are not
reportabJ,e. Section 82030(b) (2) 't' 
(In re Stone, suDra, at 57')
rhi commission stated that to be deened a gift to the city, the
gift (i.e.. reinbursenent) should satisfy at Ieast the following
four criteria:
1. The donor intended to donate the gift to
the city and not to the official;
2. The city exercises substantial control
over use of the gift;
3. The donor has not liroited use of the gift
to specified or high level enployees, but rather
has made it generally available to city personnel
in connection with city business r.rithout regard to
official 
status; and
Section 82028(a) dbfines a rrgiftrt in relevant part as:
...any payment to the extent that consideration of
equal or greater value is not received ....
Section 82044 defines a rrpaynent'r as:
...a payment, distribution, transfer, loan,
advance, deposit, gift or other rendering of noney,
property, services or anything else of va1ue,
wnetner tangible or intangible.


4. The making and use of the gift was
forrnaLized in a resolution of the city counci] -..
which enbodies the standards set forth above.
In re Stoner EllPIg r 3 FPPC ops.
at 57.
You have stated that citation Homes has offered to reirnburse
the city for the costs of airfare, neals, Iodging and other
incidental expenses. Further, the city is retaining substantial
controL over the use of the gift because the city is making the
decision as to who should travel to Southern california to view
the project. 
The developer has not li:nited use of the gifts to
spe"lfi6a high leve1 individuals and $ri1I not be involved in the
sLlection of the counc i lrnembers, planning comrnissioners or staff
menbers who will travel to southern California.
ff. in addition to the facts you have provided, the making
and use of the cost of airfare, Iodging, meals and incidental
expenses provi,ded by citation Homes is formalized in a resolution
of the city council, tnese costs would be deemed gifts to the city
and not to the councilmembers, planning commissioners and staff
members.
If the gift of travel expenses is deened a gift to the city,
the publj,c oificiats 
have no reporting requirenents since, as in
the btone opinion, any benefit they receive would be analogous to
reimbursement for expLnses or per diern from the city. 
Such items
are excluded from reporting under Section 82030(b) (2). 
Since such
items are not gifts or income to the public officials, 
they do not
give rise to any disqualification obligations under sections 871o0
and 87103.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact ne at (916) J22-59o1-
Our File No. 1 9■
-4■■
Page 4
Sincerely yours′
SCOTT HALLABRIN
Acting Ceneral Counsel
, ´
